MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-24-2015 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-10-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael H. Kim, of Michael H. Kim, APC, Tel. 650.697.8899, for plaintiffs;
Sarah R. Nichols, of Ogletree, Deakins, Nash, Smoak & Stewart, P.C., Tel. 415.442.4810, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT HERRERO BUILDERS INCORPORATED'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is denied in its entirety. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff was terminated because of his disability. At paragraphs 23 and 24 of his deposition, plaintiff declares that Tom Herrero (defendant's Superintendent of Logistics) expressly told plaintiff that he should no longer show up at the hospital worksite because plaintiff had work restrictions based on his disability. This is direct evidence of disability discrimination. Defendant objects to this evidence based on hearsay. The court overrules that objection because the evidence is an admissible admission. This evidence supports all of plaintiff's causes of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff was a qualified employee who could perform his job duties with a reasonable accommodation. "A highly fact-specific inquiry is necessary to determine what a particular job's essential functions are." Cripe v. City of San Jose (9th Cir. 2001) 261 F.3d 877, 888, fn. 12. Defendant admitted that plaintiff could perform many of his job duties with the accommodation. (Carroll Depo., 110:15-112:16). The court denies summary judgment/adjudication because there are triable issues of material fact as to all of plaintiff's causes of action. Thus, the motion is denied as to issues 1-9. The court denies summary adjudication as to the claim for punitive damages (issue 10). Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of production. Defendant does not cite any specific evidence regarding whether a managing agent, director, or officer of defendant acted maliciously. It is unclear whether Mr. Herrero qualifies as a managing agent of defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Laura Martinez, CSR No. 11332; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
